Turn 15 ‚Äî degree 18 reduces to an odd-root transgression, not a fixed tail I used only the three attachments in this message. The standing question is still whether the two quotients ŒìS,ŒìH are isomorphic over G, i.e. whether there is a continuous Œ¶:ŒìS‚ÜíŒìH with œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.The prior state is that all surviving obstruction tests have been reduced to the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 Magnus equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚àí1Y1‚àí2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. Turn 14 killed the degree-16 obstruction by using the lower I9 -base torsor, specifically the Y0‚Ü¶Y0Y4 seed, and reachedS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI18).It left degree 18 as the next possible obstruction, with square quotientL18/im‚àÇ17‚âÖL9[2],dimL9=56.The new point is that degree 18 is not analogous to degree 16. Degree 16 had root degree 8, and L8 contains genuine restricted-square material. Degree 18 has root degree 9, and 9 is odd. By the Turn-6 odd-layer mechanism,L9=[A,L8]+[B,L8],so every degree-9 primitive is already a boundary for the correction complex.LetR(Y0,Y1,q):=S(Y0,Y1,q)X‚àí2.

After Turn 14, R‚â°1(modI18). A first degree-18 tail Œ©18 is primitive, hence lies in L18. Modulo ordinary degree-17 corrections, only its square component matters:[Œ©18]‚ààQ18:=L18/im‚àÇ17‚âÖL9[2].

The restricted PBW dimensions aredimL17=7710,dimL18=14588,and therefore rank‚àÇ17=14588‚àí56=14532.So the degree-18 quotient is exactly the 56-dimensional square-root space L9[2], with no additional ordinary Lie part.Now comes the structural reduction. Because L9=im‚àÇ8, any root W‚ààL9 may be written

W=[A,U]+[B,V]for some U,V‚ààL8. Thus the relevant degree-18 moving operation is not merely the fixed high-kernel mapkerD‚â§17‚ÜíL9[2],but the tail-square transgressionT9:{continuable¬Ýdegree-9¬Ýtails}‚ü∂L9[2],W‚üºW[2].

Modulo im‚àÇ17, this map is linear: in characteristic 2,(W+W‚Ä≤)[2]=W[2]+W‚Ä≤[2]+[W,W‚Ä≤],and the cross-bracket [W,W‚Ä≤]‚ààL18 is ordinary Lie material, hence dies in Q18. Therefore, if the continuable degree-9-tail space is all of L9, then T9 is automatically onto Q18.

This is the key uniform statement of Turn 15:Degree¬Ý18¬Ýdies¬Ýonce¬Ýthe¬Ýcontinuable¬Ýdegree-9¬Ýtail¬Ýspace¬Ýequals¬ÝL9.Equivalently, the degree-18 obstruction is not a mysterious 56-coordinate class. It is the square of an odd boundary layer. The real test is whether the lower-plus-high correction tower allows arbitrary temporary degree-9 tails to be introduced and then reabsorbed through degree 17.This is exactly the structural pattern the previous turns point toward. Turn 12 warned that lower-base deformations alter the higher corrections needed later, so one must not test a fixed high-gauge class in isolation.

Turn 14 then showed that this warning was decisive at degree 16: the high torsor missed one direction, but the lower Y4 base supplied it.

Degree 18 is even more favorable because its square root L9 is already an odd boundary layer.

I am not claiming a completed proof of ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH yet. The honest status is sharper: degree 18 has been reduced from a direct 56-dimensional obstruction computation to a continuation-rank statement for degree-9 tails. If that rank is 56, then degree 18 clears, and degree 19 clears formally by the same odd-layer argument.The next computation should therefore avoid a fixed degree-18 tail. It should compute the imageC9:={degree-9¬Ýtails¬Ýarising¬Ýfrom¬Ýlower-plus-high¬Ýdeformations¬Ýthat¬Ýcan¬Ýbe¬Ýcontinued¬Ýthrough¬ÝI18}‚äÜL9.IfC9=L9,thenT9(C9)=L9[2]=Q18,so the degree-18 class can be moved into im‚àÇ17, killed by a degree-17 correction, and then degree 19 follows automatically.

ESTABLISHED: Degree 18 is governed byQ18=L18/im‚àÇ17‚âÖL9[2],dimQ18=56.

Since 9 is odd,L9=[A,L8]+[B,L8],so the degree-18 square quotient is the square of an odd boundary layer, not a new fixed-gauge tail. The obstruction reduces to the continuable-tail condition C9=L9.

GAP: I have not yet proved C9=L9. Thus I have not yet certified that degree 18 dies, and this is still not a full construction of an over-G isomorphism.

NEXT: Compute the continuation-rank mapC9‚äÜL9 for lower-plus-high deformations through I18. If dimC9=56, degree 18 and then degree 19 clear uniformly. If dimC9 <56, the missing quotient of L9[2]becomes the next genuine obstruction candidate.[DEG18-ODD-ROOT-TRANSREDUCTION]
